title: DXL logical parameter get/set + servo limits (table-aware, torque-off, read-back)
description: DXL_GET_PARAM, DXL_SET_PARAM, DXL_SET_SERVO_LIMITS over logical parameters (id/baud/return-delay/PID/limits/torque-limit/...). Table-aware: legacy MX-28 CW/CCW Angle Limit vs MX(2.0) Min/Max Position Limit. EEPROM/torque-off writes maintenance-only with torque-off confirm + read-back verify; RAM shadow update; explicit CFG_COMMIT to persist. Uses the dxlTask job queue. Native tests + api.py + vectors.
close_reason: DXL logical parameter API: DXL_GET_PARAM(0x65)/DXL_SET_PARAM(0x66)/DXL_SET_SERVO_LIMITS(0x67) on the async 0x60-0x6F job queue. New portable table src/dxl/dxl_params.{h,cpp} maps 24 logical params to table-aware addresses (legacy CW/CCW angle limits vs MX(2.0) min/max position limits). Arduino-only DxlBus readRegister/writeRegister/setTorqueOne/torqueState; executor disables torque before EEPROM writes, writes, reads back, and reports verified/VerifyFailed. 7 native test_dxl_params + 3 new test_dxl_job_api submit cases; Python builders/parsers + golden vectors. Full native 234 pass, openrb150 builds (RAM 74.4%), 78 py pass.

title: DXL scan/ping/torque/profile commands (async dxlTask job queue)
description: DXL_SCAN, DXL_PING, DXL_TORQUE, DXL_GET_SERVO_PROFILE. The DXL bus is Arduino-only and runs in dxlTask, so the API handler enqueues a job (maintenance-gated) and dxlTask executes it against DxlBus; results return via an event/telemetry frame or a polled GET_RESULT. Implements the cross-task job-queue plumbing reused by the param/register children. Native tests for the portable queue + api.py + vectors.
close_reason: Implemented the DXL maintenance command group (DXL_SCAN 0x60, DXL_PING 0x61, DXL_TORQUE 0x62, DXL_GET_SERVO_PROFILE 0x63, DXL_GET_RESULT 0x64) as an async cross-task job queue. The DXL bus is Arduino-only and owned solely by dxlTask, so apiTask must not touch it: the portable DxlJobApi enqueues a maintenance-gated job into a single-slot SPSC DxlJobQueue (submit/claim/complete/poll, volatile slot_ as the release/acquire fence); dxlTask claims one job per loop, runs it against DxlBus, and writes a serialized result back; the host polls DXL_GET_RESULT with the returned job id. Submits are gated on MacMaintenance + lock held (same chain as the maintenance targets); DXL_GET_RESULT is always allowed so a result can still be collected after the state changes. The executor reports PowerOff when DXL power is off rather than silently returning nothing. Scan returns a compact servo list (id/model/fw/proto/table), ping/get-profile return per-servo records (get-profile adds capability flags + torque + last error), torque returns the acked count. This lands the job-queue plumbing reused by the param/register children (ubs.4.4/4.5). 13 native tests (test_dxl_job_api) cover the queue mechanics and the api gating/framing with a simulated executor; full native suite 224 pass, openrb150 builds. Python builders/parsers (parse_dxl_submit / parse_dxl_result with scan decoding) + golden vectors; 72 protocol tests pass.

title: Maintenance leg/joint targets (SET_LEG_TARGET, SET_JOINT_TARGET)
description: Maintenance-gated low-speed targets: SET_LEG_TARGET (foot xyz in body frame -> body/leg IK -> joint angles -> servo ticks) and SET_JOINT_TARGET (single joint angle -> tick). Only honored in MacMaintenance with a held lock; clamped to servo travel + low speed; response reports clamp flags + IK reachability. Feeds the goal path (gated by arbiter MacMaintenance authority). Native tests + api.py + vectors.
close_reason: Implemented maintenance leg/joint targets (SET_LEG_TARGET 0x53, SET_JOINT_TARGET 0x54) as portable MaintTargetApi. SET_LEG_TARGET runs a body-frame foot (leg + x/y/z i16 mm) through BodyKinematics.solveBody + ServoMap.angleToTick, returning [result,state,reachable,clamp_low_mask,clamp_high_mask,3x tick]; an unreachable solution is reported but NOT stored so the leg never lunges to a saturated boundary. SET_JOINT_TARGET maps one joint angle (centidegrees) to a clamped tick. Both gated: honored only when live state is MacMaintenance and the maintenance lock is held (else Rejected); clamped to configured servo travel via the servo map. Stores MaintTargetSet (per-leg/joint tick + seq) for the goal-write path (still gated by MacMaintenance authority; actual bus write deferred to the goal-write activation issue). 6th api dispatcher delegate, tried after the lock handler. 9 native tests (test_maintenance_target_api) cross-check the handler against BodyKinematics+ServoMap; full suite 211 pass; openrb150 builds. Python builders/parsers + golden vectors; 64 protocol tests pass.

title: Maintenance lock commands (ENTER/EXIT/HEARTBEAT maintenance)
description: Portable MaintenanceApi command surface for the maintenance lock: ENTER_MAINTENANCE (grant token if state safe), EXIT_MAINTENANCE (release on matching token), MAINT_HEARTBEAT (refresh TTL). Token+TTL owned by the handler (USB-client construct); controlTask feeds maintenance_request/mac_lock_held into the safety FSM (MacMaintenance) and revokes on estop/disarm. ENTER response carries token; lock auto-expires without heartbeat. Native tests + api.py builders + golden vectors.
close_reason: Implemented maintenance lock command surface (ENTER/EXIT/HEARTBEAT, 0x50-0x52) as portable MaintenanceApi. ENTER grants a fresh nonzero token only in a safe live state (Disarmed/StandReady/MacMaintenance), Busy if a valid lock is held; EXIT/HEARTBEAT require matching token; lock owns token+TTL (1000ms default, auto-expiry). apiTask feeds setNow/setLiveState before handleRequest; controlTask folds lockHeld() into FSM maintenance_request/mac_lock_held and force-revokes on fault/E-stop. handleRequest now takes a 5th delegate. 11 native tests (test_maintenance_api) + full suite 202 pass; openrb150 builds. Python builders/parser + golden vectors; 60 protocol tests pass. Decomposed ubs.4 into 4.1-4.5.

title: Maintenance + DXL servo parameter commands
description: Add maintenance + DXL command IDs: ENTER/EXIT_MAINTENANCE (lock), SET_LEG_TARGET, SET_JOINT_TARGET (maintenance-gated, low-speed, clamp+reachability feedback), DXL_SCAN, DXL_PING, DXL_TORQUE, DXL_GET_SERVO_PROFILE, DXL_GET_PARAM, DXL_SET_PARAM, DXL_SET_SERVO_LIMITS (legacy CW/CCW vs MX2.0 min/max), DXL_READ_REGISTER, DXL_WRITE_REGISTER (expert-gated). EEPROM/torque-off writes maintenance-only with read-back verification. This is the 'change settings on each servo' surface. Update api.py + vectors.
close_reason: All children (4.1 maintenance lock, 4.2 leg/joint targets, 4.3 DXL scan/ping/torque/profile, 4.4 DXL logical param get/set + servo limits, 4.5 DXL raw register read/write) implemented, tested, and merged. The full maintenance + DXL servo-parameter USB command surface (0x50-0x5F lock/targets, 0x60-0x6F DXL job queue) is live with native + python tests and golden vectors.

title: Motion commands (SET_GAIT, SET_GAIT_PARAMS, SET_BODY_TWIST, SET_BODY_POSE, STOP_MOTION)
description: Add USB command IDs feeding the gait engine + body transform: SET_GAIT (stand/sit/tripod/ripple/wave/crawl), SET_GAIT_PARAMS (stride, step height, body height, duty, speed scalar), SET_BODY_TWIST (fwd/lat/yaw), SET_BODY_POSE (roll/pitch/yaw/xyz), STOP_MOTION. Only honored in motion-allowed states with arbiter authority; clamped by limits. No raw servo positions. Update api.py + vectors.
close_reason: Implemented motion command group (SET_GAIT/SET_GAIT_PARAMS/SET_BODY_TWIST/SET_BODY_POSE/STOP_MOTION) as portable MotionApi (0x34-0x38) routed via api dispatcher. Validates+clamps to gait-engine limits, stores a single MotionIntent (seq-bumped), echoes live state + motion_allowed gate; STOP always honored (zero twist + Stand). Wired into tasks.cpp (instance, handleRequest param, live-gate echo, defaults seeded from config gait). No raw servo positions. 8 native tests (test_motion_api) + full suite 191 pass; openrb150 builds (RAM 73.5%, Flash 18.3%). Python builders/parser + golden vectors; 56 protocol tests pass.

title: Safety control commands (ESTOP, CLEAR_FAULT, SET_ARMING, SET_MODE)
description: Add USB command IDs that drive the existing safety state machine: ESTOP (latch host estop -> Estop state), CLEAR_FAULT (request fault clear), SET_ARMING (arm/disarm via host when policy allows), SET_MODE (request RC_MANUAL/CONTACT_TERRAIN/etc within allowed transitions). Firmware validates against StateMachine + arbiter; RC kill always overrides. Respond with resulting state/fault. Update protocol/python api.py + golden vectors.
close_reason: Implemented safety control command group (ESTOP/CLEAR_FAULT/SET_ARMING/SET_MODE): portable ControlApi handler routed via api dispatcher (0x30-0x33); host intent (estop latch, force-disarm latch, clear-fault pulse) folded into safety state machine + new host_disarm input; live state/fault echoed in responses. Native test_control_api (6 tests) + full suite (183) pass; openrb150 build OK. Python builders/parser + golden request vectors added; 50 protocol tests pass.

title: Firmware USB control command surface (arm/gait/DXL/feature/maintenance/passive)
description: GAP: firmware api.h only exposes session (0x01-0x04), telemetry (0x10-0x13), and config (0x20-0x25) command IDs. The gait engine, leg/body IK, command arbiter, DXL bus, and safety state machine all exist internally, but there is NO USB command surface to drive them from the Mac/Jetson companion. This epic adds the remaining command groups from AGENTS.md 6.2 so a client can actually remote-control and tune the robot. MCU remains the final safety gate: every command is validated against state machine, arbiter lock, and servo limits before reaching a servo.

title: Run Phase 2 bench suspended and ground tests
description: Validate motion gradually from one leg through suspended full robot to low-speed ground tests.
acceptance_criteria: One-leg test, suspended full-body test, low-speed ground gait test, fault injection, and telemetry log review are documented.

title: Complete firmware safety state machine
description: Complete safe state transitions for boot, arm, walk, maintenance, faults, and estop.
acceptance_criteria: Boot, disarmed, arming checks, stand ready, RC manual, Jetson assisted, Mac maintenance, soft fault, hard fault, and estop transitions are tested.
close_reason: Completed firmware safety state machine. Added portable safety/state_machine.{h,cpp}: deterministic StateMachine over the 13 system_state.h States with FaultReason reporting and configurable battery cutoff. Transitions per AGENTS.md 5.3: Boot->ConfigLoad->Disarmed->ArmingChecks->StandReady->{RcManual,ContactTerrain,JetsonAssisted}, plus MacMaintenance (lock-gated), PassivePoseStream (torque-off-gated), latched FaultHard (dxl hard fault, needs explicit clear), and any-state Estop (rc kill / host estop / low battery / watchdog / rc failsafe-while-operational) with auto-recovery to Disarmed requiring re-arm. stateAllowsMotion()/stateAllowsTorque() gate goal writes and torque (passive excluded; StandReady holds torque only). Wired into controlTask (samples health/RC/arbiter inputs, publishes g_safetyState/g_faultReason/g_motionGate), dxlTask now disables torque on g_motionGate edge, i2cTask sets g_configReady after boot scan, and Health/ControlState telemetry + GET_STATUS now report the real state and fault reason. 19 host tests in test/test_state_machine cover every transition incl. estop/fault/passive/maintenance (177 native cases total pass). On-target build SUCCESS: Flash 17.4%. No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Implement rate-limited telemetry subscriptions
description: Allow host clients to subscribe to telemetry streams at safe rates.
acceptance_criteria: Host can subscribe and unsubscribe streams; stream rates are enforced; dropped frames and backlog counters are visible.
close_reason: Implemented rate-limited telemetry subscriptions. Added portable protocol/telemetry.{h,cpp} SubscriptionManager (7 streams: Health, ControlState, ServoStatus, ContactState, I2cSensorsRaw, RcInput, ApiStats) with per-stream max-rate clamps, min 1 Hz, shouldEmit() interval enforcement that counts missed slots as dropped, txBacklog counter, and SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/SET_STREAM_RATE/GET_STREAM_STATS command handling (msg 0x10-0x13). Wired into the api dispatcher (api.h/api.cpp) and into apiTask in app/tasks.cpp: handleRequest now receives &g_subs, and a non-blocking emit loop builds compact (<=256B LE) telemetry frames from the published cross-task snapshots, dropping on USB TX-full via noteTxBacklog() rather than blocking. 15 host tests in test/test_telemetry (158 native cases total pass). On-target build SUCCESS: RAM 73.3%, Flash 17.2%. No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Implement RC Jetson Mac command arbitration
description: Implement command-source priority and heartbeat-based authority management.
acceptance_criteria: RC kill overrides all; Jetson requires fresh heartbeat and RC-enabled autonomy; Mac maintenance lock expires safely.
close_reason: Implemented RC/Jetson/Mac command arbitration. Portable safety/command_arbiter (host-tested, 12 cases): single CommandArbiter owns motion authority with priority RC-kill/host-estop > Mac-maintenance-lock > Jetson > RC-manual > None. Rules: RC kill (incl failsafe/no-link, surfaced as crsf kill) or host estop denies ALL motion and immediately revokes any maintenance lock; Mac maintenance lock is token+TTL, granted only while disarmed and not killed, wins over RC/Jetson while held, expires safely when heartbeat goes stale and a late heartbeat cannot resurrect it; Jetson authority requires RC armed + RC autonomy switch (new AUX4 kChAutonomy) + fresh Jetson heartbeat (TTL); otherwise RC manual when armed, else None. crsf: added kChAutonomy(AUX4) + RcStatus.autonomy (cleared on failsafe). Wiring: controlTask evaluates the arbiter each cycle from the RC snapshot and publishes g_commandSource/g_motionAuthorized/g_killActive; dxlTask edge-triggers setTorqueAll(false) the instant authority is lost (RC kill/disarm) and keeps the goal-write path gated by g_motionAuthorized. Safe by default: DXL power + torque OFF at boot, no servos scanned, so the gate is a no-op until maintenance. Native 143/143 pass; on-target build SUCCESS RAM 72.3% Flash 16.6%. No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Implement DXL Sync Write and status telemetry
description: Update all servo goals efficiently and read status for telemetry and safety.
acceptance_criteria: All servo goals update in one control cycle; status read includes position, velocity, load or PWM, voltage, temperature, and error bits.
close_reason: Implemented DXL Sync Write + status telemetry. Portable dxl/dxl_sync (host-tested, 11 golden-vector cases): per-table-kind Goal Position addr/len (legacy 30/2, MX2.0 116/4), present-status block + Present Position addr/len, LE goal encode, status-block decode (position/velocity/load/voltage_mv/temp) incl legacy sign-magnitude (bit10) and MX2.0 signed two's-complement. Arduino DxlBus: added present_load to ServoStatus + readStatus; writeGoalPositions(targets,count) groups by control table and Sync-Writes (chunked to DXL_MAX_NODE=16, so 18 same-kind servos = 2 instructions/cycle); syncReadStatus() Sync-Reads Present Position for all servos in one packet per table (position-only because MX2.0 23B status block exceeds the 16B legacy recv buffer; full fields via per-servo readStatus round-robin); setTorqueAll(on) for arming; write_ok/fail stats. dxlTask publishes g_servoStatus via syncReadStatus each cycle (no-op until a maintenance scan populates servos; never enables torque/writes goals at boot - DXL power OFF). Goal Sync-Write activation is gated to arming/arbitration (22l.8/22l.10). Native 122/122 pass; on-target build SUCCESS RAM 71.0% Flash 13.8% (RAM jump from library sync scratch structs, noted via bd remember). No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Implement gait engine v1
description: Generate bounded foot targets for initial gait modes.
acceptance_criteria: Stand, sit, tripod, ripple, and crawl or wave produce bounded foot targets at 50 Hz.
close_reason: Implemented portable gait engine v1 (gait/gait_engine). Generates bounded foot targets in body frame B for Stand/Sit (static), Tripod (duty 0.5, groups {1,3,4}/{2,5,6}), Ripple (duty 0.667, seq 1-4-5-2-3-6), Wave & Crawl (duty 0.833, one leg at a time). Single normalized phase advances per tick at freq mapped from speed knob (0.25-1.2Hz); per-leg phase offset + duty split into stance (foot sweeps +0.5..-0.5 pushing body) and swing (returns with sin lift). Stroke vector from BodyTwist (vx/vy + yaw tangential per leg radius), all magnitudes clamped (stride<=80mm, step<=50mm, foot Z in [-120,-5]). 11 native tests: stand/sit poses, tripod group opposition, duty factors, worst-case bounded over 200-tick cycle (all 4 gaits), swing lift reaches step height, forward-twist stance direction, all targets IK-reachable via BodyKinematics (small stride), phase wrap. Native 111/111 pass; on-target build SUCCESS RAM 67.2% Flash 12.6%. Noted (bd remember) home stance is near full leg reach -> small practical stride. No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Implement 3-DOF leg IK and body pose transform
description: Implement deterministic leg inverse kinematics and body transforms for the hexapod.
acceptance_criteria: IK returns joint angles and reachability; tests cover nominal, edge, and unreachable foot targets.
close_reason: Implemented portable 3-DOF leg IK (gait/leg_ik) and body pose transform (gait/body_ik). LegIk: hip-yaw + 2-link planar (law of cosines), knee-up branch, reachable annulus with float eps, URDF-zero-relative output (home foot 127/-44.55 -> 0,0,0) feeding servo_map; FK helper for verification. BodyKinematics: body-frame B -> coxa frame using config mount XY/Z + (mount_yaw+90deg) radial relation + 21mm coxa lift (verified maps every leg's home foot to (127,0,-44.55)); REP-103 ZYX body pose via foot_B=R^T(world-t); full-chain solveBody/solveBodyPose. 14 native tests (nominal home->zero, coxa yaw, FK round-trip, full-extension boundary, too-far/too-close unreachable, clamped no-NaN, per-leg body->coxa, pose identity/yaw/translation, body-raise changes pose, invalid leg). Native 102/102 pass; on-target build SUCCESS RAM 67.2% Flash 12.6%. No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Implement servo map and joint limit enforcement
description: Convert logical leg and joint targets into DXL ticks using the validated config.
acceptance_criteria: Leg/joint target converts to DXL ticks; sign, zero, min, and max are applied; clamp flags are reported.
close_reason: Implemented servo map + joint-limit enforcement (22l.3). Added portable dxl/servo_map.{h,cpp} (no Arduino deps, host-tested): ServoMap wraps the validated RobotConfig and is the final gate between IK output and the servo bus (AGENTS.md 5.2). angleToTick(leg, joint, angle_rad) converts a URDF-zero-relative joint angle to an MX-28 goal tick via tick = center(2048) + trim_ticks + sign*round(deg(angle)*4096/360) (HexNav IK ref section 7), then clamps to the configured [min_tick, max_tick] travel and, defensively, to device range [0,4095]. Returns JointCommand{tick, clamped_low, clamped_high, unmapped} so saturation is reported (telemetry-ready for 22l.6). tickToAngle() is the inverse (applies same sign/zero) for feedback/passive-pose. Lookups servoFor(leg,joint) and servoForId(id) over the 18-entry map. Also updated the default servo id assignment in config_schema.cpp to match the real wiring per user: id = joint*kNumLegs + leg + 1 -> coxa 1-6, femur 7-12, tibia 13-18 (array stays in (leg,joint) order; only .id changed); recorded via bd remember. Acceptance met: leg/joint target -> ticks with sign+zero+min+max applied and clamp flags reported. Wired servo_map.cpp into native build_src_filter (consumers land with IK/gait 22l.4/22l.5 and sync write 22l.6). Validation: native 88/88 (10 new servo-map cases: slot/id lookup matches wiring, zero->center, +/-sign per left/right leg, trim offset, clamp high/low reported, unmapped slot, angle->tick->angle round-trip within 1 tick; plus updated schema servo-map test for the new ids); openrb150 on-target build SUCCESS (RAM 67.2% / Flash 12.6%). No git push needed (local-only Beads).

title: Implement config API over USB protocol
description: Expose config get, set, validate, commit, and reset through the host protocol.
acceptance_criteria: RAM shadow and EEPROM transaction are used; invalid configs are rejected with errors.
close_reason: Implemented the config API over the USB protocol (22l.2). Added portable config/config_api.{h,cpp} (no Arduino deps, host-tested) implementing the RAM-shadow + transactional-EEPROM-commit model: ConfigApi owns an editable serialized RAM shadow (staging_) plus a last-known-good RobotConfig, and handles the CFG_* command group (AGENTS.md 6.2) - CFG_GET_SUMMARY (schema version, payload size, block-window size, persistent+staged-valid flags, feature defaults, robot name), CFG_GET_BLOCK / CFG_SET_BLOCK (block-windowed transfer since the 331B payload exceeds one 256B frame; kCfgBlockMax=192, range-checked), CFG_VALIDATE (deserialize+validate staged bytes), CFG_COMMIT (validate -> reject if volatile -> transactional store write -> adopt as good), CFG_RESET_DEFAULTS. Invalid configs are rejected with structured results (CfgResult ValidationFailed/Volatile/CommitFailed) and malformed requests with the protocol error flag (CfgError BadRequest/BadRange). EEPROM access is decoupled behind an abstract ConfigPersistence sink so the whole module is unit-tested with the REAL transactional ConfigStore over a RAM EEPROM stand-in. Wired dispatch into the protocol layer: api.h forward-declares config::ConfigApi and handleRequest takes an optional ConfigApi* (default nullptr keeps existing call sites/tests); api.cpp delegates msg-id range 0x20-0x25 to ConfigApi, else UnknownMsg. On-target wiring respects the single-bus-owner rule (AGENTS.md 5.1: only i2cTask touches Wire/EEPROM): apiTask owns the ConfigApi and edits/validates the shadow locally with no Wire access; CFG_COMMIT hands the validated payload to i2cTask via a FreeRTOS mutex-guarded mailbox + completion semaphore and blocks bounded (<=1500ms, real commit <200ms); i2cTask performs the ConfigStore.commit transaction and, at boot, loads any valid persisted slot into a one-shot buffer that apiTask adopts once - so the ConfigApi shadow is only ever touched by apiTask. Missing/blank EEPROM keeps g_configVolatile=true so commits are rejected and defaults are used. Validation: native 78/78 (15 new config-api cases: summary persistent/volatile flags, full block round-trip equals serialized defaults, range/short-request rejects, set-block edits + length-mismatch reject, validate ok->corrupt->fail, commit persists+reloads through the real store, commit rejected when volatile/invalid, commit surfaces store-write failure, reset-defaults, adopt-payload, non-config passthrough); openrb150 on-target build SUCCESS (RAM 67.2% / Flash 12.6%). Known tradeoff: CFG_COMMIT briefly blocks apiTask during the hand-off (rare maintenance op; stub watchdog only records, no reset) - a fully async deferred-response commit could be a future refinement. No git push needed (local-only Beads).

title: Define persistent robot config schema
description: Define the EEPROM-backed schema for robot geometry, servo map, sensor calibration, and gait defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Schema includes servo ID mapping, leg geometry, servo signs/offsets/min/max, gait defaults, sensor calibration, and config version.
close_reason: Defined the persistent robot config schema (22l.1). Added portable config/config_schema.{h,cpp} (no Arduino deps, native-tested): RobotConfig RAM-shadow struct covering all acceptance fields — config schema_version, robot identity, shared leg link lengths (L_COXA/L_FEMUR/L_TIBIA), per-leg coxa-mount geometry (x/y/z + home yaw), the full 18-servo map (DXL id -> leg+JointRole, sign, trim_ticks, min/max tick clamps), gait defaults (body height, stride, step height, duty, speed, GaitId), per-foot contact-sensor calibration (baseline + near/touch/load thresholds + enabled), and a feature-default bitmask. Geometry/servo defaults sourced from HexNav_description IK reference (mount positions section 3, link lengths section 4, sign map section 7): center 2048/180deg, conservative +/-90deg travel [1024,3072], contact sensing OFF until calibrated, all optional features off — the compiled SAFE fallback for missing/blank EEPROM (AGENTS.md 1.3/4.3). Explicit little-endian field-by-field serialize/deserialize (endian-stable, padding-independent, version-tagged, kConfigPayloadSize=331B << config-store 2032B slot, static_assert guarded) plus validateRobotConfig (unique ids, in-range legs/joints/ticks, +/-1 signs, min<max, non-zero links/body-height, every leg/joint slot covered exactly once). Designed to plug straight into ConfigStore commit/load (serialized payload) for issue 22l.2. Wired config_schema.cpp into native build_src_filter. Validation: native 63/63 (12 new schema cases: defaults valid, servo map/sign/geometry/gait, serialize size + buffer-too-small, full round-trip, reject bad length/version/dup-id/bad-ranges/missing-slot); openrb150 on-target build SUCCESS (RAM 63.0% / Flash 10.6%). No git push needed (local-only Beads).

title: Phase 2 - Firmware motion, sensors, config API, and telemetry
description: Make the robot controller useful without the companion app: safe walking, servo mapping, IK/gait control, sensor polling, config updates, telemetry, and command arbitration.

title: Implement CRSF parser and RC failsafe status
description: Parse ExpressLRS CRSF channel frames and expose RC/failsafe status.
acceptance_criteria: Channels normalize to stable units; stale RC triggers failsafe flag; arming, kill, and gait channels are mapped.
close_reason: CRSF parser + RC failsafe (rbg.9). Added portable src/input/crsf_parser.{h,cpp} (no Arduino deps, native-testable): CRSF DVB-S2 CRC8 (poly 0xD5); byte-wise streaming Parser state machine (sync 0xC8 / length / data) validating framing+CRC and unpacking the 16x11-bit RC_CHANNELS_PACKED (type 0x16) payload; ticksToMicros normalization (172..1811 -> ~988..2012us). RcStatus mapping normalizes channels to microseconds and maps AETR + AUX layout: arm(CH5)/kill(CH6)/3-pos gait(CH7); evaluateFailsafe raises failsafe when no valid frame within 250ms and forces disarm+kill; initRcStatus boots to safe failsafe state. Wired into tasks.cpp rcTask: file-static crsf::Parser g_crsfParser + crsf::RcStatus g_rcStatus, Serial2 @420000 baud (guarded by PIN_SERIAL2_RX so mkrzero still builds), drains bytes -> applyFrame, evaluateFailsafe every loop; rcTask owns Serial2 exclusively. Native build_src_filter includes input/crsf_parser.cpp; added test/test_crsf (8 cases: crc8 KAT 0xD3, ticks->us bounds, full-frame decode roundtrip, resync-after-garbage, bad-CRC reject, arm/kill/gait mapping, stale failsafe, safe init). Validation: native 36/36; openrb150 builds RAM 61.0% / Flash 9.3%.

title: Implement 24LC32 page driver and transactional config store
description: Implement EEPROM page IO and two-slot CRC-protected config transactions.
acceptance_criteria: Config load, validate, commit, power-cycle recovery, corrupt-slot rejection, and defaults fallback are tested.
close_reason: 24LC32 driver + transactional config store (rbg.8). Added portable src/config/config_store.{h,cpp} (no Arduino deps, native-testable) with a ConfigBackend abstraction: two redundant slots (slot0@0, slot1@2048 in the 4KB device), 16-byte header (magic 'HEXC', version, sequence, length, payload_crc, header_crc) using protocol CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE. load() returns newest valid slot (highest sequence); commit() always writes the INACTIVE slot with sequence+1, payload first then header last, so a crash mid-commit never corrupts the active slot (power-cycle safe). Validation rejects bad magic/version/oversize-length, bad header_crc, and bad payload_crc; inspect() reports per-slot validity. Added Arduino-only src/config/eeprom_24lc32.{h,cpp}: Eeprom24LC32 implements ConfigBackend over Wire @0x50, read auto-chunked to 32B, write split on 32-byte page boundaries with ACK-poll write-completion (no fixed delay), 4KB bounds-checked. Wired into tasks.cpp i2cTask: file-static g_eeprom(Wire)+g_configStore+g_configVolatile; at boot, if EEPROM present, inspect slots and set g_configVolatile = no valid slot (defaults-fallback hook; full payload schema lands in Phase 2). Native build_src_filter includes config/config_store.cpp; added test/test_config_store (9 cases: blank->load fails, commit/load roundtrip, A/B slot alternation + newest wins, corrupt-newest falls back to older, corrupt-header rejected, power-loss-during-commit keeps previous (fault-injected fake), oversize rejected, header serialize roundtrip, empty payload). Validation: native 51/51; openrb150 builds RAM 63.0% / Flash 10.6%.

title: Implement DYNAMIXEL bus manager maintenance scan
description: Create the single-owner DXL bus manager and maintenance-safe scan/ping path.
acceptance_criteria: Scan, ping, and status work with torque off; bus errors are counted; no other task touches DXL directly.
close_reason: Single-owner DXL bus manager (rbg.6). Added src/dxl: portable dxl_model.{h,cpp} (ServoProfile, TableKind, model->table classification: MX-28 legacy=29 Protocol 1.0 CW/CCW angle limits, MX-28(2.0)=30 Protocol 2.0 min/max position limits + sync/fast-sync/profile-velocity/bus-watchdog capability flags) and Arduino-only dxl_bus.{h,cpp} (DxlBus owns Serial1 via Dynamixel2Arduino; maintenance-safe ping w/ protocol 2.0->1.0 fallback, range scan, torque-off readStatus for position/velocity/voltage/temperature/hw-error; BusStats counters scans/pings/reads/last_error; NEVER enables torque or DXL power). Wired one file-static dxl::DxlBus g_dxlBus(Serial1) into tasks.cpp dxlTask (begin() once at boot, no auto-scan since scan needs power on -> deferred to Phase 2 maintenance API); no other task touches Serial1/DXL. Native build_src_filter now includes dxl/dxl_model.cpp; added test/test_dxl_model (6 cases). Validation: native 28/28 (6 dxl_model + 10 api + 12 protocol); openrb150 builds RAM 60.6% / Flash 9.1%.

title: Implement USB API v0
description: Expose the first USB CDC API commands needed by host tools.
acceptance_criteria: HELLO, HEARTBEAT, GET_STATUS, and GET_CAPABILITIES work over USB CDC.
close_reason: USB API v0 implemented over USB CDC: HELLO, HEARTBEAT, GET_STATUS, GET_CAPABILITIES (msg IDs 0x01-0x04) plus a generic unknown-msg error response. Added protocol/api.{h,cpp} (handleRequest dispatch, DeviceInfo/StatusSnapshot inputs, versioned response payloads) and protocol/frame_reader.{h,cpp} (streaming COBS frame reassembly with auto-reset and back-to-back delimiter sharing). apiTask now owns USB CDC exclusively (removed health task Serial prints), drains Serial bytes through FrameReader, builds a live StatusSnapshot (uptime/state/dxl_power/dxl_power_control/battery_mv/watchdog_missed) and replies. Mirrored host module protocol/python/hexapod_protocol/api.py with build_command/parse_* helpers. Shared golden vectors regenerated in frames.json. Tests: native Unity 22/22 (12 protocol + 10 api/frame_reader), Python pytest 43/43, both cross-validated against the same golden bytes. openrb150 builds RAM 58.3% / Flash 8.8%.

title: Implement protocol framing golden vectors
description: Implement COBS plus CRC16 framing and shared protocol test vectors.
acceptance_criteria: COBS plus CRC16 encode/decode works in firmware and host tests; corrupt frames are rejected.
close_reason: COBS + CRC16 framing implemented and tested on both sides. Firmware: src/protocol/{crc16,cobs,framing}.{h,cpp} (portable, heap-free, caller-owned buffers; 14-byte LE header per AGENTS.md 6.1, CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE). Host: protocol/python/hexapod_protocol. Shared golden vectors in protocol/tests/vectors/frames.json (generated by gen_vectors.py). Native Unity tests (pio test -e native) 12/12 pass; Python pytest 34/34 pass; both assert identical golden bytes and reject corrupt frames (bit flip, bad magic, truncation, malformed COBS). Added [env:native] + [fw_base] refactor to platformio.ini. protocol/README.md documents format + test commands.

title: Bring up FreeRTOS task skeleton
description: Start the firmware RTOS tasks with bounded stacks and visible health reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Control, DXL, API, I2C, RC, and health tasks start; stack high-water marks are visible; watchdog stub exists.
close_reason: FreeRTOS task skeleton up (briscoetech/FreeRTOS_SAMD21). Six bounded-stack tasks created in src/app/tasks.cpp (control/dxl/rc/api/i2c/health) with priorities+periods in src/app/task_config.h. Health task blinks USER LED and reports uptime, per-task stack high-water marks, loop counts, and watchdog mask over USB CDC. Software watchdog stub in src/safety/watchdog.{h,cpp} (HW WDT pet TODO). Task stacks allocated boot-only; no runtime heap churn. Both envs build (openrb150 RAM 56.5%/Flash 8.4%). Runtime [health] observation folded into HIL smoke tests rbg.10.

title: Implement board HAL and safe boot defaults
description: Centralize board pins and safe boot behavior.
acceptance_criteria: DXL power remains off at boot; board pins/constants are centralized; battery ADC and status hooks exist.
close_reason: Board HAL added: src/board/openrb150_pins.h centralizes pins; src/board/board.{h,cpp} forces DXL power FET OFF at boot, exposes setDxlPower/setUserLed status hooks and battery ADC (readBatteryMilliVolts, provisional 4.0x divider flagged for HIL calibration in rbg.10). main.cpp routed through HAL. Both envs build clean (openrb150 RAM 10.9%/Flash 5.6%).

title: Create monorepo skeleton and firmware build target
description: Create the monorepo layout and make the OpenRB-150 firmware build from the command line.
acceptance_criteria: Repo layout exists; firmware compiles for OpenRB-150; README contains flash command; local check command is documented.
close_reason: Custom OpenRB-150 PlatformIO board (boards/openrb150.json) + exact variant (variants/OpenRB-150) created; firmware builds via 'pio run' and was flashed to hardware. README documents flash + local check commands.

title: Phase 1 - Firmware foundation and safe I/O
description: Boot OpenRB-150 firmware safely, establish bus ownership, prove DXL/I2C/RC/API basics, and create a validated configuration store.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: DXL raw register read/write (expert-gated diagnostics)
description: DXL_READ_REGISTER, DXL_WRITE_REGISTER: raw address/length access for diagnostics. Expert-gated (disabled by default / compile or runtime expert flag), maintenance-only, torque-off for EEPROM regions, read-back verified, logged. Uses the dxlTask job queue. Native tests + api.py + vectors.
close_reason: DXL raw register read/write expert diagnostics: DXL_READ_REGISTER(0x68)/DXL_WRITE_REGISTER(0x69) on the async 0x60-0x6F job queue. Raw address/length access bypassing the logical table, expert-gated (DxlJobApi.setRawRegisterEnabled, default off, reset clears; firmware enables only under HEXAPOD_ENABLE_DXL_RAW_REGISTER compile flag) on top of the maintenance lock+MacMaintenance gate. Write flags bit0 marks EEPROM region so the executor disables torque first; both write and read-back verify (VerifyFailed on mismatch). 5 new native test_dxl_job_api cases; Python builders/parsers + golden vectors. Full native 238 pass, openrb150 builds, 82 py pass. Completes ubs.4 (children 4.1-4.5 all done).

title: Firmware: enrich servo_status payload (torque_enable, current/PWM, torque/goal limits)
description: User wants full per-servo info: measured position, set target, torque, temperature, etc. servo_status today encodes only 13 bytes/servo: id,pos,vel,load,volt,temp,err -- it DROPS the torque_enabled flag already in dxl::ServoStatus and never reads present current/PWM or torque/goal-current limits. Add to the read + wire payload: torque_enable (1b), present_current/PWM (2b, MX2.0 Present Current / legacy via load), torque_limit (legacy 0x22) / goal_current (MX2.0), and surface goal_position so a client sees commanded vs measured. Keep 18-servo payload within 256B (consider splitting into servo_status base + servo_status_ext, or a per-id detail-on-request response). Update tasks.cpp builder, telemetry.h, protocol/python ServoStatus dataclass decoder + golden vectors. Coordinate goal/target with eax.2 servo_goals.

title: Companion: kinematic model + animated hexapod widget (live + replay)
description: Build a hexapod pose model fed by joint_state telemetry (or servo_status+servo_map fallback) and an animated representation: start with a fast 2D top-down + side view widget (pyqtgraph) showing all 6 legs / 18 joints updating live, then load the HexNav URDF (robot_ros_simulation/HexNav_description/urdf/HexNav.urdf) for a 3D view. Must animate smoothly from live telemetry and from replayed sessions without blocking the UI thread. This is the user-facing 'animate a representation' deliverable; feeds nxi.4 URDF viewer.

title: Protocol python: RobotConfig/servo-map decoder + tick<->angle helpers + joint_state decode
description: Add a RobotConfig dataclass decoder (mirrors config_schema.h: links, leg geometry, 18 ServoConfig sign/trim/min/max, gait defaults, foot cal, feature defaults) parsed from CFG_GET_BLOCK payloads, plus tick<->angle conversion helpers (center 2048, 4096 ticks/rev, sign, trim). Add decode for the new joint_state/servo_goals/leg_state streams. UI-independent + golden vectors. Fallback path that converts servo_status raw ticks->angles when joint_state stream is unavailable.

title: Firmware: joint_state telemetry stream (mapped per-leg/joint angles)
description: Add a new StreamId (append, do not renumber) that emits, for all 18 joints, the leg index, joint role, and present joint angle in a fixed unit (e.g. millidegrees or centidegrees) derived from servo_map (sign, trim_ticks, center 2048, 4096 ticks/rev). This gives the companion ready-to-render joint angles without duplicating tick math or needing the full config. Must work in both active and passive (torque-off) modes. Update telemetry.h kNumStreams, payload builder in tasks.cpp, protocol/python telemetry.py decoder + golden vectors.

title: Live joint-pose telemetry + animated hexapod model
description: GOAL (user): companion receives a position for every joint of the hexapod and animates a live representation. GAP: telemetry only has servo_status = raw present_position ticks per DXL id, with NO leg/joint identity and NO angle conversion. AGENTS.md 6.3 also specs servo_goals, leg_state, and passive_joint_state streams that are NOT implemented (only 7 streams: Health/ControlState/ServoStatus/ContactState/I2cSensorsRaw/RcInput/ApiStats). And the python protocol package has CFG msg ids but no RobotConfig/servo-map decoder, so a client cannot map id->(leg,joint) or convert ticks->radians. This epic adds the joint-pose data pipeline end-to-end and the animated model widget. Enables nxi.4 (URDF viewer).

title: UI: Servo Monitor + DXL Tuning page (live status table + per-servo settings)
description: Live servo_status table (id/pos/vel/load/voltage/temp/error) for all 18 servos, plus per-servo logical parameter editor: read profile, edit CW/CCW or min/max limits, PID, torque limit, return delay; stage/diff/write/read-back/commit. Raw register editor expert-gated. Complements nzi.5 Servo Config (map/EEPROM).

title: UI: Mode & Safety Center page (arm/disarm/clear-fault/mode + feature toggles)
description: New page (or upgrade ModeSafetyPage): arm/disarm, clear fault, set mode, enter/exit maintenance + passive, feature-flag toggle cards with availability/reason. Buttons show required state/lock; dangerous ops staged+confirmed+logged. Global estop already in safety bar.

title: Client + Qt service: typed control methods, real ESTOP/arm/disarm wiring
description: Add typed methods to ProtocolClient for each control group; wire them into ConnectionService (QObject) as worker-thread calls. Replace the event-only emergency_stop TODO with the real ESTOP command + ack handling. Add arm/disarm/clear-fault/set-mode, set-gait, dxl param read/write helpers exposed to the GUI thread.

title: Protocol: command builders/parsers for all control groups + golden vectors
description: Extend protocol/python/hexapod_protocol/api.py with build_/parse_ helpers for safety (estop/clear_fault/set_arming/set_mode), motion (set_gait/set_gait_params/set_body_twist/set_body_pose/stop_motion), feature (get/set/reasons/reset), maintenance+DXL (enter/exit_maintenance, set_leg/joint_target, dxl_scan/ping/torque, get_servo_profile, get/set_param, set_servo_limits, read/write_register), contact/sensor, and passive groups. Add golden vectors matching firmware. UI-independent.

title: Companion full remote-control + monitoring coverage
description: Ensure the Mac companion can remote-control AND monitor everything: arm/disarm/estop/mode, gait + body motion, per-servo DXL telemetry AND settings writes, feature toggles, foot-contact sensors + calibration, and passive pose. Today the protocol python client + ConnectionService only cover session/telemetry/config; emergency_stop is an event-only TODO. This epic adds the client builders, CLI, Qt service wiring, and the missing control/monitor UI pages. Depends on firmware command surface (ubs).

title: Passive pose mode commands (PASSIVE_ENTER, PASSIVE_EXIT, PASSIVE_SET_STREAM_RATE, PASSIVE_ZERO_REFERENCE)
description: Add USB command IDs for torque-off passive pose streaming: enter (disarmed/maintenance-safe, all torque off, no goal writes, present-position stream), exit, set stream rate, zero reference. Reject motion/gait/IK while active. Update api.py + vectors.

title: Contact/leveling + sensor control commands
description: Add USB command IDs: CONTACT_ENABLE/DISABLE/CALIBRATE/SET_THRESHOLDS, LEVELING_ENABLE/DISABLE/SET_PARAMS, I2C_SCAN, I2C_GET_TOPOLOGY, SENSOR_GET_STATUS, SENSOR_CALIBRATE, SENSOR_SET_RATE. Firmware rejects when sensors missing/stale. Update api.py + vectors.

title: Feature flag commands (FEATURE_GET, FEATURE_SET, FEATURE_GET_REASONS, FEATURE_RESET_DEFAULTS)
description: Add USB command IDs to read/toggle runtime feature flags (foot_contact, terrain_leveling, sensor_polling, jetson_control, passive_pose). Firmware may reject/auto-disable with a reason when hardware/state/safety disallow. Mirrors feature_flags.h. Update api.py + vectors.
close_reason: Feature flag command group (0x39-0x3C): FEATURE_GET/FEATURE_SET/FEATURE_GET_REASONS/FEATURE_RESET_DEFAULTS over the USB protocol. New portable FeatureApi stores the host's desired enable set while the control task publishes per-feature availability + reason each cycle; effective enabled = desired && available, so the firmware can reject enabling an unavailable feature (reason echoed) and auto-disable a feature when its hardware/state disappears without losing host intent (AGENTS.md 1.3). Covers foot_contact, terrain_leveling, sensor_polling, jetson_control, passive_pose; sensor_polling is the one fully wired runtime toggle (drives g_sensorPollingEnabled), the rest report honest reasons (DependencyOff/NotImplemented) pending ubs.5/ubs.6/jetson. 11 native test_feature_api cases; Python builders/parsers (FeatureList/FeatureReasons/FeatureSetResult) + golden vectors. Full native 249 pass, openrb150 builds (RAM 74.4%), 91 py pass.

title: Step 11 - UI smoke tests + end-to-end validation + README
description: pytest-qt smoke tests for service + widgets + pages + window construction; full 'uv sync' + pytest + 'hexapod-cli ports' + headless app launch. Finalize companion/README.md. Acceptance: all companion tests pass; app and CLI run; README complete; close 3of.1/3of.4/3of.5/3of.7/nzi.1.

title: Step 10 - Main window + app entry (Dracula shell)
description: MainWindow: NavRail + SafetyBar + QStackedWidget pages + EventStrip, wired to ConnectionService; app.py main() applies Dracula theme and shows window. Acceptance: app launches headless (offscreen), theme applied, estop visible on all pages. Satisfies nzi.1.

title: Step 9 - Core pages (connect/overview/mode-safety/diagnostics)
description: BasePage chrome + ConnectPage (port discovery/connect/fw info), OverviewPage (live badges), ModeSafetyPage (subscriptions+estop), DiagnosticsPage (raw feed). Acceptance: each page constructs with a ConnectionService under offscreen Qt.

title: Step 8 - UI widgets (nav rail, safety bar, event strip, estop)
description: Reusable widgets: StatusBadge, EmergencyStopButton, SafetyBar (connection/mode/arming/torque/source/rc/battery + global estop), NavRail (sections+items), EventStrip. Dracula-styled object names. Acceptance: widgets instantiate under offscreen Qt.

title: Step 7 - Qt connection service bridge
description: ConnectionService (QObject): wraps ProtocolClient, re-emits telemetry/connection/status as Qt signals marshalled to the GUI thread, connect/handshake on a worker thread, status QTimer poll, safe no-op commands when disconnected. Acceptance: service constructs and connect/disconnect cycle works against FakeStream under offscreen Qt.

title: Step 6 - Dracula theme module
description: theme/: official Dracula palette, comprehensive QSS stylesheet, apply_theme(app) with Fusion + QPalette. Lazy PySide6 import. Acceptance: apply_theme runs under offscreen Qt without error.

title: Step 5 - CLI tools
description: Typer CLI: ports/status/stream/log/stream-stats/gui, auto-port pick. Acceptance: 'uv run hexapod-cli ports' and '--help' work; commands import cleanly. Satisfies 3of.7.

title: Step 4 - Session logger + raw replay
description: SessionLogger writes session.json + raw_frames.bin + telemetry.jsonl + events.jsonl; iter_raw_frames reader. Acceptance: round-trip test writes records and reads frames back without hardware. Satisfies 3of.5.

title: Step 3 - Threaded typed protocol client
description: ProtocolClient: background reader thread, seq-matched request/response, telemetry dispatch, connection callbacks, hello/status/capabilities/subscribe/unsubscribe/stream-stats. Acceptance: tests drive a FakeStream loopback and verify request/response correlation + telemetry decode. Satisfies 3of.4.

title: Step 2 - Transport: frame extractor + serial link
description: Pure FrameExtractor (0x00-delimited reassembly), SerialLink over pyserial, and list_serial_ports/open_serial. Lazy serial import so tests need no hardware. Acceptance: tests/test_frame_extractor.py passes (split frames, multi-frame, garbage resync).
close_reason: Transport validated. FrameExtractor (0x00-delimited reassembly) hardened with discard-until-next-delimiter resync on overflow; SerialLink + list_serial_ports/open_serial use lazy pyserial import (no hardware needed for tests). 5 tests pass: single frame, split across chunks, multi-frame per chunk, leading-bytes-form-junk-frame, runaway overflow drop + resync.

title: Step 1 - Scaffold + environment install
description: Stand up companion/ as an installable package. Pin a PySide6-compatible Python (<3.14), 'uv sync' the companion + hexapod-protocol path dep, and confirm the two entry points import. Acceptance: 'uv sync' succeeds; 'uv run hexapod-cli ports' runs; companion/README.md documents install/run/test/lint. Satisfies 3of.1.
close_reason: Scaffold validated: pinned Python 3.12 (.python-version), added companion/justfile (just launch/sync/cli/ports/test/lint/fmt) and companion/README.md + protocol/python/README.md. 'uv sync --extra dev' installs companion + editable hexapod-protocol + PySide6 6.11.1. 'uv run hexapod-cli ports' runs; all entry points import (hexapod_companion 0.1.0). 'just --list' shows recipes.

title: Companion app incremental build and validation
description: Step-through epic that lands the companion app in small, independently verifiable increments instead of one big drop. Each child issue is a single reviewable step: write -> test -> validate -> close, then move to the next. Mirrors the code already drafted under companion/ but sequences it so each layer is proven before the next is claimed. Parent feature issues 3of.1/3of.4/3of.5/3of.7/nzi.1 are satisfied collectively by these steps.

title: Implement calibration workflows
description: Create guided calibration for servo geometry and sensor baselines.
acceptance_criteria: Servo zero, sign, min/max, leg geometry, and sensor baselines can be calibrated, validated, saved, and exported.

title: Build Diagnostics page
description: Create developer diagnostics for raw frames, protocol, DXL, I2C, and firmware timing.
acceptance_criteria: Raw frame inspector, protocol stats, DXL errors, I2C errors, and firmware timing counters are visible.

title: Build Servo Config page
description: Create EEPROM-backed servo map and config editor UI.
acceptance_criteria: User can view, edit, validate, diff, commit, export, and import servo map/config.

title: Build Leg Lab page
description: Create individual leg and joint maintenance controls.
acceptance_criteria: Individual leg, foot, and joint controls work only in maintenance mode and show IK and clamp feedback.

title: Build Gait Lab page
description: Create safe controls for gait selection and body movement.
acceptance_criteria: Gait selection and safe body twist controls work with visible arming and mode requirements.

title: Build Connect and Overview pages
description: Create connection and status pages for daily development.
acceptance_criteria: User can connect, see firmware capabilities, robot state, battery, DXL/I2C health, and active command source.

title: Build PySide6 application shell
description: Build a native-style application shell with navigation and global safety affordances.
acceptance_criteria: App launches on macOS with navigation, global status bar, and global estop widget.

title: Phase 4 - Companion UI foundation and core pages
description: Build the first usable macOS development UI with clear navigation and safe controls.

title: Implement companion CLI tools
description: Add CLI commands for status, streaming, logging, replay, and config transfer.
acceptance_criteria: status, stream, log, replay, config dump, and config restore commands work.

title: Implement session logger
description: Record raw frames, decoded telemetry, events, and config snapshots.
acceptance_criteria: Logger writes a complete session directory with raw frames, decoded telemetry, events, and config snapshot.

title: Implement typed protocol client
description: Expose high-level Python methods for the robot serial API.
acceptance_criteria: Supports hello, status, capabilities, subscribe, config get, and safe error handling.

title: Implement serial connection service
description: Create non-blocking USB serial connection lifecycle management.
acceptance_criteria: Discovers USB serial ports, connects, reconnects, heartbeats, and reports link state without blocking.

title: Implement Python protocol codec
description: Implement COBS, CRC, framing, and schema-version handling in Python.
acceptance_criteria: Codec matches firmware golden vectors; corrupt frames are rejected; schema version detection works.

title: Create Python companion package scaffold
description: Set up the companion Python package and command entry point.
acceptance_criteria: Package installs locally; lint and test commands are documented; CLI entry point exists.

title: Phase 3 - Companion transport, protocol client, and data logger
description: Create a scriptable Python package that connects to the robot, decodes telemetry, sends safe commands, and records sessions before building the full UI.

title: Implement Robotic Finger Sensor v2 polling
description: Poll all six muxed finger sensors without blocking control.
acceptance_criteria: All six mux channels produce pressure, proximity, and contact telemetry at configured rate; I2C errors do not block control.
close_reason: Implemented Robotic Finger Sensor v2 polling + contact estimation. Portable sensors/contact_estimator (host-tested, 9 cases): per-foot debounced state machine AIR/NEAR/TOUCH/LOADED/RELEASE/STALE/FAULT from VCNL4040 proximity + LPS25HB pressure-delta vs baseline, with touch/load/release debounce counts, release hysteresis (fraction of touch thresh), consecutive-failure FAULT, stale-timeout decay, slow baseline drift while unloaded, confidence + loadedMask. Thresholds/baseline/enable come from config::FootSensorCal (disabled until calibrated). Arduino-only sensors/finger_sensor: VCNL4040 PS power-up (16-bit) + PS_DATA read, LPS25HB WHO_AM_I check + CTRL_REG1 active + 24-bit PRESS_OUT auto-increment read; every transaction bounded and returns false on failure (never blocks). i2cTask polls ONE present mux channel per iteration (round-robin, exclusive one-hot select->read->deselect), lazily configures each board, forces reconfigure after a failed read (board reseat), runs tickStaleness, and republishes g_footState[6] snapshot. Runtime g_sensorPollingEnabled toggle (default on). Control loop never blocked (separate task, bounded work). Native 131/131 pass; on-target build SUCCESS RAM 72.2% Flash 14.8%. No git push (local-only Beads).

title: Create Phase 1 hardware-in-loop smoke tests
description: Document and script the initial hardware smoke checks.
acceptance_criteria: Checklist covers boot, USB, DXL, I2C, EEPROM, CRSF, and fault observations.
close_reason: Phase 1 HIL smoke tests (rbg.10). Added tools/hil_smoke.py: automated USB-API smoke driver using the shared hexapod_protocol Python package + pyserial. Auto-detects/opens the USB CDC port, runs HELLO (verifies protocol v0.1 + device identity 'OpenRB150-Hex'), GET_STATUS (asserts boot-safety invariants: DXL power OFF at boot, watchdog_missed==0, telemetry decodes), HEARTBEAT x2 (asserts uptime advances = scheduler running), GET_CAPABILITIES (feature bitmap decodes); per-check PASS/FAIL, exit 0 all-pass / 1 fail / 2 setup-error; --list and --port options with single-port auto-detect. Verified offline: imports clean, --list enumerates ports, bad-port fails gracefully (no hardware needed). Added docs/hil_smoke_phase1.md: full checklist covering boot (USER LED blink, DXL LED red/power-off), USB handshake (scripted), DXL bench scan (model/protocol detect, torque-off read-only, error counters), I2C topology (mux 0x70/EEPROM 0x50/foot channels 0-5 Present/Fault/Missing, reserved 6-7, mux-removal degradation), EEPROM persistence (commit/power-cycle/reload, corrupt-slot fallback, volatile-defaults reject-commit), CRSF (channel normalize, arm/kill/gait map, TX-off failsafe within ~250ms), and fault observations (estop state transition, watchdog, runtime I2C removal degrades not freezes). Closes the Phase 1 epic gate. Validation: native 51/51 still green; script verified without hardware.

title: Implement TCA9548A and root I2C scan
description: Create I2C mux selection and scan tooling for root and mux channels.
acceptance_criteria: Root bus scan finds mux and EEPROM; channel scan finds sensor devices; mux channel selection is exclusive.
close_reason: TCA9548A + root I2C scan (rbg.7). Added portable src/sensors/i2c_topology.{h,cpp} (no Arduino deps, native-testable): address constants (mux 0x70, EEPROM 0x50, VCNL4040 0x60, LPS25HB 0x5C), I2cTopology/ChannelInfo model, FootSensorState classification (Present=both on-board devices, Fault=partial, Missing=neither), isExpectedRootAddress, updateChannelState, footSensorPresentMask/Count over foot channels 0..5 (reserved 6,7 excluded). Added Arduino-only src/sensors/i2c_bus.{h,cpp}: I2cBus owns Wire (arduino::TwoWire), begin(100kHz); probe via beginTransmission/endTransmission==0; selectChannel writes one-hot bitmask to TCA9548A (exclusive, exactly one channel live); selectNone; scanRoot detects mux+EEPROM; scanChannels selects each foot channel exclusively, probes VCNL4040/LPS25HB, classifies, always deselects after; scanAll = root then channels; I2cStats counters (root/channel scans, mux_select_errors, last_error). Wired into tasks.cpp i2cTask: file-static i2c::I2cBus g_i2cBus(Wire) + g_i2cTopology, begin()+scanAll() one-time boot discovery (blocking probes kept off control loop); i2cTask owns Wire exclusively. Native build_src_filter includes sensors/i2c_topology.cpp; added test/test_i2c_topology (6 cases: init clears, classify all 4 combos, expected root addr, updateChannelState, present mask+count, reserved-channel exclusion). Validation: native 42/42; openrb150 builds RAM 63.0% / Flash 10.2%.

title: Companion: per-servo detail inspector (position/target/torque/temp/current/error)
description: Per-servo detail view (drill-down from the servo_status table in kj8.5): show measured position, commanded target, torque-enable state, present current/PWM, load, torque limit, voltage, temperature, and decoded hardware-error bits, with live update + history sparkline. Consumes the enriched servo_status (eax.6) + servo_goals (eax.2). Complements kj8.5 servo table and nzi.5 servo config.

title: Firmware: leg_state telemetry stream (foot targets + IK reachability)
description: Add leg_state stream (AGENTS.md 6.3): per-leg foot xyz target, IK result, and reachability/clamp flags. Lets the animation show foot targets and unreachable poses. Append StreamId, update builder + python decoder + vectors.

title: Firmware: servo_goals telemetry stream (commanded angle + clamp flags)
description: Add servo_goals stream (AGENTS.md 6.3): per-servo commanded joint angle/tick after IK + clamp flags, so the companion can overlay commanded vs present pose in the animation. Append StreamId, update builder + python decoder + vectors.

title: UI: Passive Pose page
description: Enter/exit torque-off passive mode, set stream rate, zero reference; live joint positions from passive present-position telemetry; record posed sessions for calibration/URDF.

title: UI: Foot Contact & Leveling page
description: Per-leg contact state (AIR/NEAR/TOUCH/LOADED/RELEASE/STALE/FAULT), live proximity/pressure/delta/confidence, threshold editor + calibrate, enable/disable contact + leveling with reasons. Consumes contact_state + i2c_sensors_raw streams.

title: CLI: control + tuning subcommands
description: Add Typer subcommands: arm, disarm, estop, clear-fault, mode, gait set/params, twist, feature get/set, contact enable/disable/calibrate, passive enter/exit, dxl scan/ping/torque/get/set/limits. Each validates safe state and prints firmware response.

title: Complete operator and developer documentation
description: Write user-facing and developer-facing project docs.
acceptance_criteria: Docs cover wiring, flashing, config, calibration, operation, troubleshooting, logging, plotting, and URDF use.

title: Add CI and quality gates
description: Add repeatable local and CI checks.
acceptance_criteria: Firmware unit tests, protocol vectors, Python tests, linting, and type checks run in CI where possible.

title: Implement Jetson Python bridge
description: Use the same protocol client on Jetson for high-level commands and telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Jetson can send body twist/gait commands and receive telemetry using the same protocol client.

title: Phase 6 - Jetson bridge, integration, packaging, and release hardening
description: Integrate autonomy workflows and make the tools repeatable for daily use.

title: Build URDF Viewer page
description: Render the URDF and update it from live or replay joint telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Model renders and updates from live or replay joint telemetry.

title: Create hexapod URDF package
description: Create a URDF model for body, legs, and actuated joints.
acceptance_criteria: URDF loads successfully and includes links and joints for body, six legs, and 18 actuated joints.

title: Add event markers and session notes
description: Record and visualize user and robot events in sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Gait changes, faults, config commits, and user notes appear on plots and in logs.

title: Build Plot Workbench page
description: Create live and replay plotting for robot telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Live and replay plots can display selected fields from servo, leg, control, RC, and sensor streams.

title: Phase 5 - Plotting, session analysis, and URDF visualization
description: Turn the companion app into the main troubleshooting and calibration tool.

title: Add UI smoke tests and replay fixtures
description: Make UI pages testable without hardware by using replay fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: Pages construct in tests and can consume replay telemetry without hardware.

title: Build Sensor Dashboard page
description: Create live sensor panels for the muxed Robotic Finger Sensor v2 boards.
acceptance_criteria: All six sensor channels show live values, health, and calibration actions.

title: Implement replay system
description: Replay saved sessions into services and tests without hardware.
acceptance_criteria: Replay can feed decoded telemetry to tests and future UI from saved sessions.

title: Package companion app for local macOS install
description: Create a documented local install/package path for the Mac app.
acceptance_criteria: Documented install and run flow works from a clean Mac developer machine.

title: Implement Mac to Jetson proxy mode
description: Allow Mac companion access through Jetson when direct MCU USB is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Mac companion can monitor or control through Jetson when MCU USB is owned by Jetson.

title: Prototype optional ROS 2 bridge
description: Prototype ROS 2 integration on Jetson only.
acceptance_criteria: ROS 2 node maps high-level messages to serial API without changing MCU firmware.

title: Implement session export reporting
description: Export selected data and summaries from recorded sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Selected session data can export to CSV and a human-readable summary report.

value: gait home-stance reach margin: the documented HOME_FOOT stance (mid legs at coxa radial 127mm, d=83.7mm) sits only ~7.6mm from full leg extension (L2+L3=91.37mm). For the mid legs (3,6) whose radial direction aligns with body +X, forward stride extends them radially, so practical forward stride at the default 40mm ride height is < ~7mm before IK becomes unreachable. Real walking needs a retracted home stance / lower ride height, or per-leg stride scaling. Gait engine clamps targets to a safe box but does NOT guarantee IK-reachability at large stride.

USB command surface gap: firmware api.h only implements session(0x01-0x04)+telemetry(0x10-0x13)+config(0x20-0x25). The gait engine/IK/arbiter/DXL bus/state machine exist internally but have NO USB command IDs. Remote control (arm/disarm/estop/mode, gait/motion, DXL param writes, feature toggles, maintenance leg/joint, contact/sensor, passive pose) is unimplemented end-to-end. Firmware epic ubs adds the command surface; companion epic kj8 (deps ubs) adds protocol builders+client+CLI+UI. Monitoring is mostly already covered by telemetry streams (servo_status, contact_state, i2c_sensors_raw, rc_input, health, control_state).

DXL sync RAM cost: Dynamixel2Arduino legacy ParamForSyncWriteInst_t (~277B) + RecvInfoFromStatusInst_t (~321B) scratch structs are DxlBus members -> on-target RAM jumped 67.2%->71.0% (23.3KB/32KB). DXL_MAX_NODE=16 and DXL_MAX_NODE_BUFFER_SIZE=16 on SAMD21, so legacy syncRead recv buffer can't hold the 23-byte MX2.0 full-status block -> syncReadStatus reads Present Position only (fits: legacy 2B@36, V2 4B@132); full status fields come from per-servo readStatus() round-robin. 18 servos > DXL_MAX_NODE(16) so a same-kind group sync write chunks into 2 instructions per cycle.

Phase 2 HIL bench/ground test procedure documented in tools/hil_phase2.md (one-leg, suspended, ground, fault-injection, telemetry-review). hexapod_src-22l.11 stays OPEN until the physical motion tests are actually run on hardware and signed off.
DXL bus default protocol is 1.0 (MX-28AT factory/legacy table). DxlBus.ping tries 1.0 then 2.0; readStatus defaults to 1.0 when a servo's protocol is unknown.

Default DXL servo id wiring: id = joint*6 + leg + 1 (0-indexed). Coxa ids 1-6, femur 7-12, tibia 13-18. So leg1={1,7,13}, leg2={2,8,14}, leg3={3,9,15}, leg4={4,10,16}, leg5={5,11,17}, leg6={6,12,18}. RobotConfig.servos[] array stays in (leg,joint) order; only the .id field follows this map. Set in config_schema.cpp defaultRobotConfig().
